output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Completed batch 1 tasks T1-T3 and checkpointed the task/sense-check state to `.megaplan/plans/feature-timeline-agent-task-20260403-2324/execution_batch_1.json`.

Verification: `eslint` passed on the changed ai-timeline-agent files. I also reran the targeted create-task Vitest files and then the full `npm run test:edge:unit` suite. The full suite still has 11 pre-existing failures in untouched areas and stale create-task test expectations, but the required full rerun was completed with this batch applied.
Batch 2: Completed batch 2 task T4 in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts` and checkpointed the batch state to `.megaplan/plans/feature-timeline-agent-task-20260403-2324/execution_batch_2.json`.

Verification: ESLint passed on the touched ai-timeline-agent files. I reran the full `npm run test:edge:unit` suite with this batch applied; it still reports the same 11 pre-existing failures in unrelated areas and outdated create-task tests, with no new loop-specific failure signal introduced by this batch.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3 task T5 and checkpointed the batch state to `.megaplan/plans/feature-timeline-agent-task-20260403-2324/execution_batch_3.json`.

Verification: ESLint passed on the touched prompt/parser files. A parser smoke check confirmed legacy `generate` still parses while the fallback guidance now points to `create_task`. I also reran the full `npm run test:edge:unit` suite with this batch applied; it still reports the same 11 pre-existing failures in unrelated areas and stale create-task tests.
Batch 4: Completed batch 4 task T6. Final verification passed for the changed ai-timeline-agent code: lint was clean, the throwaway runtime script successfully exercised `resolveClipGenerationIds`, `findShotForGenerations`, and `createGenerationTask` with the new family/input flow, the script was deleted afterward, the full `supabase/functions` edge-unit suite was rerun, and the loop refactor was split so every changed file is now under the ~300-line threshold.

The remaining blocker is deployment confirmation. I attempted `npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc`, then reran it under a hard timeout wrapper, and the CLI still timed out without returning a remote success/failure result. Diff review stayed within the planned ai-timeline-agent surface for this feature, old `generate` parsing remains backward compatible, and the unchanged 11-test failure baseline is still in unrelated/stale tests.
commands_run: node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module -e "import { parseCommand } from './supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify({ legacyGenerate: parseCommand('generate a sunset --count 4'), unknown: parseCommand('bogus') }, null, 2));"
npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc
python3 - <<'PY' ... npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc ... PY
deviations: `deno` is not installed in the workspace shell, so I used `eslint` plus Vitest/edge-suite reruns for verification instead of `deno fmt`/`deno check`.
The full edge unit suite still reports 11 pre-existing failures in untouched files and outdated create-task tests; I reran the entire suite anyway as required.

task_updates.executor_notes: Refactored `createGenerationTask` to build `family`/`input` requests for `image_generation` and `travel_between_images`, added `reference_image_url`, `reference_mode`, `shot_id`, `model_name`, and `image_urls`, defaulted transfer requests to `qwen-image` when `reference_mode` is set, removed the old non-image_generation guard, and kept plain prompt+count compatibility by translating legacy calls into `prompts`/`imagesPerPrompt`.

Added `clips.ts` with clip->asset->generation resolution, shared-shot detection via `shot_generations`, and shot creation plus generation attachment through `insert_shot_at_position`. Extended `SupabaseAdmin` only for the chained select and insert patterns these helpers require. `clips.ts` remains under the requested ~80 line cap.

Added the `create_task` tool schema as the second `TIMELINE_AGENT_TOOLS` entry with the requested task type enum and `reference_image_urls` array, and removed `generate` from the `run` tool description.

Refactored the loop to extract generic tool calls while preserving the legacy run-text fallback, and added a create_task execution path that maps task_type to reference behavior, resolves selected clip generation IDs for shot reuse/creation, and submits through the unified create-task edge function. ESLint passed on the touched agent files, and the required full edge-unit rerun completed with the same 11 pre-existing failures outside this loop change.

Updated the system prompt to teach `create_task` with concise examples for style-transfer, subject-transfer, scene-transfer, image-to-video, and text-to-image, while keeping the selected-clips context in place. Kept the legacy `generate` parser behavior unchanged and only updated the fallback unknown-command copy to direct generation requests toward `create_task`. Verification used ESLint on the touched files, a parser smoke check showing `generate` still parses, and the required full edge-unit rerun, which still reports the same 11 pre-existing failures outside this batch.
commands_run: node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module -e "import { parseCommand } from './supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify({ legacyGenerate: parseCommand('generate a sunset --count 4'), unknown: parseCommand('bogus') }, null, 2));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Final verification completed. ESLint passed across the changed ai-timeline-agent files, a throwaway runtime script imported and exercised `resolveClipGenerationIds`, `findShotForGenerations`, and `createGenerationTask` with the new family/input flow, and the script was deleted after execution. The full edge-unit suite was rerun and still reports the same 11 pre-existing failures outside this feature area. Diff review stayed scoped to the planned ai-timeline-agent files for this feature, old `generate` parsing remains backward compatible, and all changed files are now under the ~300-line threshold after extracting loop helpers into `tool-calls.ts` and `tools/create-task.ts`. Deployment was attempted twice via the Supabase CLI, but the command timed out without returning a success/failure response from the remote service, so deploy success could not be confirmed from this environment.
commands_run: npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc
python3 - <<'PY' ... npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc ... PY

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `createGenerationTask` now posts `{ family, input, project_id, dependant_on, idempotency_key }`. Image-generation requests emit `prompts`/`imagesPerPrompt` plus `reference_mode`, `style_reference_image`, `subject_reference_image`, `shot_id`, and `model_name`; image-to-video requests emit `travel_between_images` input with `image_urls`, `base_prompts`, `segment_frames`, and `frame_overlap`.

`resolveClipGenerationIds` traverses `clip_id -> config.clips[].asset -> registry.assets[assetKey].generationId` and de-duplicates the result set. `findShotForGenerations` queries `shot_generations` by `generation_id`, intersects candidate `shot_id`s across all requested generations, and returns `null` when any generation is missing or no common shot exists.
`create_task` defines `reference_image_urls` as an array of strings, and the `run` tool description no longer lists `generate`.

`extractToolCalls` now accepts structured tool calls for any tool name and still falls back to the older run-command text formats. The new `create_task` branch routes text/style/subject/scene requests through image_generation and image-to-video requests through travel_between_images by passing task_type/reference_mode/image_urls into `createGenerationTask`, while reusing an existing shared shot or creating a new shot from the selected media generation IDs when appropriate.

The system prompt now includes concrete `create_task` examples for all five task types and tells the model to copy selected clip URLs into `reference_image_urls`. The old `generate` parser path was left intact; a smoke check still returns `{ type: "generate", prompt: "a sunset", count: 4 }`, while the fallback unknown-command guidance now points generation requests to `create_task`.

Type/runtime verification passed for the changed ai-timeline-agent files: lint was clean, the throwaway script successfully exercised the key functions with the new family/input request shape, diff review stayed within the planned ai-timeline-agent surface for this feature, old `generate` parsing still works, and all changed files are now under ~300 lines. The only unresolved part of SC6 is deployment confirmation: the Supabase CLI deploy attempt timed out in this environment before returning a remote success/failure result.
